title: Vision-Language Models, End to End
tagline: From CLIP-style alignment to instruction tuning — the full VLM training stack with a live mini-CLIP
summary: How a modern VLM is built (vision encoder + projector + language model) and the four training stages that get it there: contrastive image-text alignment, image-grounded language pretraining, instruction tuning, and preference alignment. A mini-CLIP runs live in your browser to make the contrastive part concrete, and we close with the tool-use angle behind ThermEval and the calibration trap real VLMs fall into on thermal data.
highlights: Diagram of the modern VLM stack (vision encoder → projector → LLM) with named example components.
All four training stages explained with what changes (data, loss, frozen vs trained parts).
Live mini-CLIP: tiny image and text encoders trained in-browser on 4 toy categories; similarity matrix updates each step.
The tool-use trap: why VLMs fail on thermal-color → temperature calibration, and how thervlm-style tool calls fix it.
